description_long: This is a small, marble-clad bank building with a classical architectural design, featuring a prominent triangular pediment supported by four large cylindrical columns at the front. The structure sits on a raised base, with a central entrance framed by an arched decorative element and flanked by the columns. The roof is steeply pitched and appears to be constructed from the same marble material as the rest of the building, giving it a cohesive and monumental appearance. The sides and rear of the building are solid, with no visible windows or secondary entrances, emphasizing security and solidity. The overall form is symmetrical, with clean lines and a polished stone finish that reflects traditional neoclassical influences. The entrance is the only visible point of access, suggesting a secure and formal environment typical of financial institutions.
description: A small, marble neoclassical bank with a triangular pediment, four front columns, and a single central entrance. The building is solid, symmetrical, and exudes security and formality.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, four large columns, and the triangular pediment above.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid marble wall and the alignment of the four columns along the side.
description_view_2: Rear view with no visible entrance or windows, only the columns and the back wall are visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the solid wall and columns.
attribute_entrance_design: Single central door, likely wood, framed by an arched decorative element and flanked by two large marble columns on each side; no visible handles or glass panels.
